fullArticle:
BEIJING, July 1 (Xinhua) -- The inaugural freight train of China Railway Express (CR Express), departing from Chongqing in southwest China to Duisburg, Germany, in 2011, established a high-speed logistical link between western China and Europe.

Fifteen years later, the railway service has grown into a rail network spanning much of Eurasia, linking hundreds of cities across China, Europe and Asia.

Over the past decade, the railway service has evolved into far more than a freight corridor. It has enabled more inland Chinese cities to engage in international trade, provided businesses along the route with more efficient logistics solutions, and fostered closer economic ties between China and Europe.

The express service is one of the flagship infrastructure projects under the Belt and Road ...
